# The Cumulus Community

## Freely Choosing Cooperative Efficiency

**The Cumulus Community has two sister corporations as its foundation to contribute to solving
modern polycrisis: one for profit, the other not.**

1. Our LLC, [_SubtleTea Solutions (STS)_](https://subtleteasolutions.com), is a
   computational and organizational science services company dedicated to
   realizing lucrative yet sustainable business models.
2. Our nonprofit, the [_Generation of Participation in Democracy
   (GPD)_](https://gpdamericas.org) is registered with the Internal Revenue
   Service (IRS) as a **501(c)(3) tax-exempt public charity** and a California
   Public Benefit Corporation meeting all associated [compliance
   requirements](https://www.oag.ca.gov/system/files/media/Guide%20for%20Charities.pdf)---see
   also [CalNonprofits _Nonprofit Compliance
   Checklist_](https://calnonprofits.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/CA-Compliance-Checklist-Revised-02-2024.pdf).
  - **IRS Employer Identification Number (EIN): 41-4992916**
  - The GPD's first _Flagship Organization_ is the [_Instituto
    Dourados_](https://institutodourados.org). The _Instituto's_ mission is to
    provide computational epidemiological tools for public health planning that
    will be owned, maintained, and further developed by Indigenous communities
    in the _Grande Dourados_ region in Mato Grosso do Sul, Brazil, and beyond.


![](images/matty-linkedin-banner.png){fig-align="center" width=525px
style="margin-top: 15px; margin-bottom: 15px;"}

## Governance

The Cumulus Community is guided by a shared governance model designed to
promote transparency, continuity, and responsible stewardship across our
affiliated organizations and projects.

Our [Governance](governance.qmd) page introduces the [Board of
Directors](governance.qmd#board-of-directors), explains our current governance
structure, and provides access to [governance
documents](governance.qmd#governance-documents-archive): meeting minutes and
other institutional records. We believe that durable institutions are built
through clear responsibilities, documented decisions, and an ongoing commitment
to openness and thoughtful evolution.


## Strategic Organization and Communication {style="margin-top: 25px;"}

We focus on developing an approach to social change that strategically
balances economic cooperation and competition, guided by a new
integrative approach to social science.

We are staunchly anti-ideology. We will never seek to force conformity.

Our
system is founded on the belief that all people should be free to choose for
themselves based on their own self interest. Education, therefore, is essential
so that one can independently confirm that they _chose_ what they want, which
requires knowing how to tell good from bad in an ever more complex world.

In the Cumulus Community we focus on developing beneficial practices as antidotes
to occupy the cultural niche otherwise occupied by relatively harmful ones.
Democratic change tends to focus on influencing and winning elections,
petitioning government, protest, and other overt forms of struggle.

Our operating hypothesis takes a different approach: we believe we can each
do better individually if we design and willingly adopt strategic social
coordination policies.

---

<br>

![](images/cumulus-community-lettermark.svg){width=150 .centered}
<!-- style="margin-left: auto; -->
<!-- margin-right: auto; display: block;"} -->

<br>


{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
